Results driven Mechanical Engineer with excellent technical, analytical, and communication skills. Oversaw implementation of AS9100 and ISO9001 Quality Management Systems. Managed relocated product distribution activities.

SALES Professional Summary  
Graduated from high school and enlisted in the United States Air Force with honorable discharge. Started working in a small lumber company where I became a volunteer firefighter and then EMT for 20 years. Changed careers in 1994 to work as an EMT at a healthcare facility, began college to enter nursing school in 1995, and graduated in 2001 with an Associate of Applied Science in Nursing. Currently working at the same healthcare facility now as a nurse, with experience in various departments including surgery. Pursued additional training in emergency care and worked in surgery, with primary interest in cardiovascular surgery.

In August 2015, transitioned to travel nursing after 21 years of service. Worked at multiple hospitals on travel assignments, including a short-term position at another healthcare facility, and continued working in various capacities across different states.

Skill Highlights  
- Laparoscopic procedures familiarity  
- Medication administration expert  
- Vascular care understanding  
- Advanced Cardiac Life Support (ACLS) certification  
- Computerized charting specialist  
- Enthusiastic caregiver  
- IV drug therapy management  
- Specimen collection/processing proficiency  
- Sterilization techniques mastery  
- OR and ER experience  

Accomplishments  
- Collaborated with physicians to plan and implement patient care.  
- Maintained compliance with regulatory standards.  
- Educated families about procedures, treatment regimens, and prevention.  
- Documented patient intake information and health-related history.  
- Ensured quality control through admissions, assessment, treatment, and referrals.  
- Treated patients with trauma, acute chest pain, respiratory failure, and drug overdoses.  
- Properly disposed of biohazard waste.  
- Educated patients about medical procedures, recovery measures, and medication instructions.  
- Consistently praised for efficient assistance in surgical procedures.  
- Prepared patients for surgery by evaluating vitals and administering medication.  

Professional Experience  
- Sales Warehouse Foreman  
  Company Name, City, State  
  10/1981 to 07/1994  
  Responsibilities included ordering materials and operating forklifts.  

- Sales Office Clerk  
  Company Name, City, State  
  07/1976 to 10/1981  

- EMT/Registered Nurse  
  Company Name, City, State  
  07/1994 to 08/2015  
  Started as EMT, transitioned to registered nurse circulator in surgery.  

- Office Clerk  
  Company Name, City, State  
  05/1975 to 06/1976  
  Served in various supporting roles during enlistment in military service.

- Operate specialized equipment in the cardiac departments.

- Ensure all necessary documents and blood products are ready before surgery.

SALES Professional Summary  
Graduated from high school and enlisted in the United States Air Force with honorable discharge. Started working in a small lumber company where I became a volunteer firefighter and then EMT for 20 years. Changed careers in 1994 to work as an EMT at a healthcare facility, began college to enter nursing school in 1995, and graduated in 2001 with an Associate of Applied Science in Nursing. Currently working at the same healthcare facility now as a nurse, with experience in various departments including surgery. Pursued additional training in emergency care and worked in surgery, with primary interest in cardiovascular surgery.

In August 2015, transitioned to travel nursing after 21 years of service. Worked at multiple hospitals on travel assignments, including a short-term position at another healthcare facility, and continued working in various capacities across different states.

- Laparoscopic procedures familiarity

- Sterilization techniques mastery  
- OR and ER experience

- Maintained compliance with regulatory standards.  
- Educated families about procedures, treatment regimens, and prevention.  
- Documented patient intake information and health-related history.  
- Ensured quality control through admissions, assessment, treatment, and referrals.  
- Treated patients with trauma, acute chest pain, respiratory failure, and drug overdoses.  
- Properly disposed of biohazard waste.  
- Educated patients about medical procedures, recovery measures, and medication instructions.  
- Consistently praised for efficient assistance in surgical procedures.  
- Prepared patients for surgery by evaluating vitals and administering medication.

Proven leader with solid performance seeking new challenges to bring a comprehensive set of skills to your company. Former military leader, certified instructor, designated a Subject Matter Expert in 2 fields of medicine, experienced public speaker.  

Skill Highlights  
Skills Used: Microsoft Office, Smarttech SMART Board, Public Speaking, Public Relations, Marketing, Sales  

Instructor/Writer - Combat Medic Advanced Individual Training  
1st Detachment, 108th Training Command - November 2014 to October 2015  
- Train, lead, and mentor upcoming healthcare specialists.  
- Be the example of what right looks like for new healthcare practitioners to follow.  
- Read, revise/edit, publish and teach course material for a healthcare program that graduates over 30,000 individuals annually.  
- Project management regarding training program tasks.  
- Supervise the logistics of monthly synchronization meetings from catering and event planning to security and logistics for monthly gatherings of key leaders in public venues.  
- Teach NREMT curriculum to students attempting EMT certification.  
- Evaluate NREMT practical skills for EMT certifications.  
- Teach CPR/BLS.  
- Teach TCCC core curriculum and advanced skills, including mass casualty management, patient administration, disaster management, critical thinking, leadership development, convoy operations, logistics, supply chain management, and other necessary skills to be a Healthcare Specialist in the US Army.  

Instructor/Writer - Infantry Battlefield First Aid  
Alpha Company, 2nd Infantry Battalion - November 2012 to November 2014  
- Teach TCCC core curriculum and First Aid to classes of up to 220 individuals.  
- Teach additional skills such as mass casualty management, critical thinking, leadership development, medical evacuation logistics, and other necessary skills to save lives.  
- Supervise records management of instructor credentials, supply orders, equipment maintenance, and building/site maintenance.  

Emergency Care Sergeant, Squad Leader  
Echo Company, 2nd Infantry Battalion - April 2011 to November 2012  
- Hospital, Pre-Hospital, Long-term Geriatric Population, VA Home/Clinic, Acute Care, Phlebotomy/Lab, Immunization Clinic, Medical/Surgical Ward, PACU, ICU, Pediatric Emergency Department, Telemetry, Behavioral Health Ward.  
- Supervised 2 noncommissioned officers and 10 soldiers.  
- Assigned them to 5 ambulance teams.  
- Managed their duties and helped resolve their personal issues to make them effective leaders and efficient medics.  
- Responsible for medical equipment sets valued at over 100,000.00 each including vehicles and HAZMAT.  
- Designated as the primary Information Technology point of contact within the section, implemented and oversaw the transition to paperless office management and streamlined previously cumbersome communications methods.  

Emergency Care Specialist  
Headquarters and Headquarters Company/Bravo Company, 1-41 Infantry Battalion - June 2009 to June 2011  
- Responsibilities included clinical patient care, austere/remote medical care, and providing medical support for training exercises.  
- Conducted individual and crew-served weapons training, and infantry tactics and SOPs.  

Skills Used: Microsoft Office 2013, Emergency Management Systems, Network Administration, DSLR Photography, Supply Chain Management, Classified Document Management, Joint Commission Inspection Standards, Classroom Instruction.  

Core Accomplishments  
- Designed and implemented a mass casualty evacuation SOP for a battalion of 600 soldiers; adopted as the brigade standard for 3000 soldiers.  
- Designed and implemented a field expedient casualty evacuation system using civilian vehicles.  
- Coordinated resources to create a cost-effective, fully equipped aid station and casualty evacuation plan using non-medical assets.  
- Developed a training program using scenario-based training for managing mass casualty situations.  

Emergency Care Specialist  
Charlie Company, 302nd Brigade Support Battalion - June 2007 to June 2009  
- Cross-trained personnel in U.S. tactics and medical skill sets.  
- Managed integration and training in Medical Communications for Combat Casualty Care (MC4) system for a company of 170 medical personnel.  
- Planned and implemented a full spectrum evacuation of families during Operation Courageous Channel.  

Emergency Care Specialist  
Training Support Company, 32nd Medical Brigade - May 2005 to June 2007  
- Coached, mentored, and credentialed individuals in medical skills.  
- Provided medical support for 50,000 personnel annually in a training area of 27,990 acres.  
- Supervised pharmaceutical supply chain management.  
- Managed logistics for a special needs Intensive Care Unit during refugee movements.  

Professional Experience  
Healthcare Consultant - 05/2016 to 10/2016  
- Introduced patients to the training program and assisted with EEG monitoring.  
- Set up and troubleshot equipment and software.  
- Coached individuals through initial and follow-up brain mapping.  

Healthcare Consultant - 02/2016 to 05/2016  
- Provided detoxification services in an international medical center.  
- Monitored individuals during treatment and educated them about health decisions.  

Medical Investment Management:  
- Consulting for high-net-worth individuals on healthcare and lifestyle changes.  
- Achievements include being selected as Science Advisor for a provincial level tumor research institute partnership.  

English Teacher - 10/2015 to 01/2016  
City Best Learning English -  
- Taught English language skills to children using curriculum for grades Pre-K1 to G6.  
- Developed lesson plans and improved public relations with local clients.  

Education  
High School Diploma - 2004  

Personal Information  
Service Country: United States  
Branch: Army  
Rank: Sergeant - 2004 to October 2015  
Honorable Discharge  
Willing to relocate: Anywhere  

Interests  
- Highly experienced in leading, training, and developing others.  
- Proven motivator, leader, trainer, and builder of high-energy teams.  

Skills  
Academic, Acute Care, Adobe Acrobat, Adobe Photoshop, Behavioral Health, Coach, Consulting, CPR, Critical Thinking, Document Management, Emergency Care, English, First Aid, Instruction, Investment Management, Logistics, Marketing, Project Management, Public Relations, Public Speaking, Supply Chain Management.  

Additional Information  
- Active DoD Secret security clearance.  
- Available ASAP.  
- Authorized to work in the US for any employer.

January 2013 to Current  
[Company Name]  
Natural Resource Specialist GS-12/5  
I manage approximately 98 range units, which consists of tribal, allotted, government tracts, and taken area. Duties require calculating Animal Unit Months (AUMs), determining accurate acreage, establishing conservation plans, invoicing, modifying and adjusting yearly rental rates, issuing 10-day show cause letters for non-payment, and cancellation letters as necessary. I work cooperatively with Tribal Natural Resource staff, members of the Natural Resource Committee, and Tribal Council. I provide technical assistance as requested to the Three Affiliated Tribes, updating or amending Grazing/Natural Resource Resolutions, assisting in projects, determining taken area acres (Non-Trust on-off acres). Most recently, partnered with the Tribe under a 93-638 contract to complete a rangeland inventory providing technical assistance in grazing study. This established accurate range unit boundaries and allotments and updated stocking rates, AUMs, and identified infested areas. Prepared for a new permit period required updating grazing resolutions, sending out over 18,000 Authorities to Grant Grazing Privileges to individual Indian interest owners, and managing the application and allocation process. Established 5-year range unit permits once allocated. Prepared and wrote notices for non-compliance, non-payment, trespass livestock, and other notices to permittees, landowners, and the tribe. Directed staff in inspecting, sending notices, and taking corrective action on trespass livestock on the range units and farm and pasture leases. Currently, managing 700 producing wells, approximately 400 are in the range unit or farm and pasture leases. Responsible for identifying, planning, and withdrawing oil gas right-of-way from existing surface leases. Investigated, determined trespass, took corrective action, and assessed penalties on oil or saltwater spills on trust property. Assisted with oil and gas/environmental mitigation. Mediated and consulted during disputes within the range unit or farm and pasture lease involving permittees, landowners, and oil companies. Provided assistance in locating allotments to landowners using ArcGIS and fieldwork. Reviewed acquisition and disposal conveyance documents for accuracy. Responded to data calls from regional and central offices, gathering and compiling documentation. Provided documentation for Appeals as requested. Assigned as alternative coordinator for the Agency Freedom of Information Act (FOIA). Supervised and directed duties to natural resource staff and Agency staff when acting as Superintendent. Other supervisory duties included updating position descriptions and rating performance appraisals. Took corrective action on non-performing and/or misconduct employees as necessary. Acted as Superintendent in absence.

Duties require calculating Animal Unit Months (AUMs), determining accurate acreage, establishing conservation plans, invoicing, modifying and adjusting yearly rental rates, issuing 10-day show cause letters for non-payment, and cancellation letters as necessary. I work cooperatively with Tribal Natural Resource staff, members of the Natural Resource Committee, and Tribal Council. I provide technical assistance as requested to the Three Affiliated Tribes, updating or amending Grazing/Natural Resource Resolutions, assisting in projects, determining taken area acres (Non-Trust on-off acres). Most recently, partnered with the Tribe under a 93-638 contract to complete a rangeland inventory providing technical assistance in grazing study. This established accurate range unit boundaries and allotments and updated stocking rates, AUMs, and identified infested areas. Prepared for a new permit period required updating grazing resolutions, sending out over 18,000 Authorities to Grant Grazing Privileges to individual Indian interest owners, and managing the application and allocation process. Established 5-year range unit permits once allocated. Prepared and wrote notices for non-compliance, non-payment, trespass livestock, and other notices to permittees, landowners, and the tribe. Directed staff in inspecting, sending notices, and taking corrective action on trespass livestock on the range units and farm and pasture leases. Currently, managing 700 producing wells, approximately 400 are in the range unit or farm and pasture leases. Responsible for identifying, planning, and withdrawing oil gas right-of-way from existing surface leases. Investigated, determined trespass, took corrective action, and assessed penalties on oil or saltwater spills on trust property. Assisted with oil and gas/environmental mitigation. Mediated and consulted during disputes within the range unit or farm and pasture lease involving permittees, landowners, and oil companies. Provided assistance in locating allotments to landowners using ArcGIS and fieldwork. Reviewed acquisition and disposal conveyance documents for accuracy. Responded to data calls from regional and central offices, gathering and compiling documentation. Provided documentation for Appeals as requested. Assigned as alternative coordinator for the Agency Freedom of Information Act (FOIA). Supervised and directed duties to natural resource staff and Agency staff when acting as Superintendent. Other supervisory duties included updating position descriptions and rating performance appraisals. Took corrective action on non-performing and/or misconduct employees as necessary. Acted as Superintendent in absence.

October 2008 to January 2013  
[Company Name]  
Soil Conservationist GS-0457-11/4  
Responsible for the management and development of conservation plans for trust lands on the reservation. Managed land classifications for farm and pasture leases, rights-of-way, and conveyance using ArcGIS. Worked cooperatively with federal and state agencies and the tribe on conservation projects and protection of trust lands. Updated, established and implemented conservation plans for expiring farm and pasture leases. Inspected, monitored utilization, and ensured proper management of all farm and pasture leases. Developed requirements which were amended into the land use bill. Coordinated a bio-control project on invasive species. Provided maps, legal descriptions, and technical assistance to landowners and lessees. Performed fire duties when needed.

June 2006 to October 2008  
[Company Name]  
Rangeland Management Specialist GS-0454-09  
Conducted rangeland inventory in conjunction with the Natural Resource Conservation Services, requiring identification of plants and ecological sites. Digitized range data and calculated AUMs and stocking rates using ArcGIS. Involved in the conversion of systems, encoding AUMs, rental rates, tracts, and legal descriptions. Wrote conservation plans to meet goals and objectives for the range program. Monitored range units, determining use and trend.

August 2005 to June 2006  
[Company Name]  
Range Technician GS-0455-06  
Conducted compliance and monitoring of range units on the reservation. Counted and identified brands in trespass situations. Inspected and monitored range unit boundaries. Assisted in land status questions and map requests.

[Company Name]  
Soil Conservationist GS-458-05  
Conducted line transects for ranchers, identifying and documenting native grasses and shrubs for trend and condition assessment.

May 1999 to September 2003  
[Company Name]  
Natural Resource Specialist (student trainee) GS-454-4  
Conducted compliance checks, wrote Environmental Assessments for oil and gas wells, participated in rehabilitation of abandoned oil wells, and helped manage conservation projects.
